summary: Quipu del Mani II -- intermediate tier of the Incahuasi peanut tribute accounting system (UE 11). 330 cords (289 L1 + 37 L2 + 4 L3) organized in 107 tiny clusters (60 triplets + 24 singletons + 10 doubles + 9 pentads) -- the most fragmented structure in the analyzed corpus. Shares the nickname 'Quipu del Mani' with UR270 (UE 13). Unique structural signature: 9-cord EMPTY opening spacer (p1-p9), then ~200 cord-pairs of paired W (gross deposit) + NB (net after deduction) implementing Urton & Chu's (2015) deposit formula directly at the cord-pair level, closing with 14+13 empty cords at p251-264 and p277-289. Minimal cadastral vocabulary (9 words: piqa x4, taqa x4, chaqa x3, maqa x2, chiqa x2, qaqa x2, waqa x2, paqa, chaqaqa) used as provenance labels indexing ~100 regional peanut deposits; NO ritual vocabulary (pama, taki, chama all absent -- contrast with UR270 Zone 3). Deepest Merkle cascade in the Incahuasi set (depth 4) with lowest covering ratio (0.16) -- a narrow-tall audit hierarchy. Low Benford match (0.57) confirms round quota values, not raw tallies. Sits between UR270 (warehouse day-book) and UR268 (district summary) in the three-tier peanut tribute pipeline.
interpretation:
ATTRIBUTION (prior work vs v3 novelty)
  PRIOR WORK: (a) UR269 as the 'Quipu del Mani II' -- Urton field nickname; (b) UE 11 peanut storeroom context (Urton-Chu 2015); (c) the peanut pipeline tier structure established by Urton across UR270 (UE 13), UR269 (UE 11), UR268 (UE 18).
  V3 CONTRIBUTION: (1) quantification of UR269 as tier-2 REGIONAL AGGREGATOR via 17 Ascher sums with max cascade depth 4 (deepest Merkle of the Incahuasi set); (2) ALBA syllabary reading of merged-corpus knots; (3) Benford match quantifying the tier position between UR270 (day-book) and UR268 (district summary).

IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-14)
  KH0506 = UR269. Nickname (OKR): 'Quipu del Mani' (second khipu with this nickname alongside UR270).
  330 cords (289 L1 + 37 L2 + 4 L3). Architecture: 1-PAIRE depth L3.
  107 clusters: 60 triplets + 24 singletons + 10 doubles + 9 pentads + 2 quartets + 2 sextets. ZERO 9-cord clusters (distinguishes from UR278's 26 and UR270's 7).
  4 colors aggregated: NB(106), W(81), AB(64), MB(52). KFG reports 17 unique colors.
  Excavation: Sector A, Subsector 1, CA_, UA 4, Unidad 20N, UE 11 (distinct from UR270=UE13, UR277=UE17, UR278=UE16 within the same Sector A complex).
  Museum: Proyecto Arqueologico Incahuasi, n. 13828.
  Cited in Urton & Chu 2015 (LAQ 26/4); Urton 2017; Medrano & Khosla 2024.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  AGREED=78, KFG_RESOLVED=110, OKR_ONLY=141 (subsidiaries + unmatched), DIVERGENT=1.
  OKR/KFG agreement 0.41.
  v1 -> v3: 2 -> 21 readable STRING (+19), 1 -> 9 vocabulary (+8), 110 L? filled.

ASCHER LAYER (diagnostic profile of an intermediate aggregator)
  17 pendant-pendant sums parsed (KFG catalog reports 43 total: 21 primary + 10 inter-group + 7 colored + 1 subsidiary).
  Max cascade depth = 4 -- DEEPEST Merkle tree of the four Incahuasi siblings (vs UR278 depth 2, UR277 depth 3, UR270 depth 3).
  covering_ratio = 0.16 -> LOW capacity (45/289 L1 involved). LOWEST coverage of the Incahuasi set.
  0/17 checksums verified (OKR S-knot transcription issue, same as UR270/UR277).
  Pass 3A/3C/3D: 0/0/0.
  Benford match = 0.5685 (LOW). UR269 = standardized quota values (2000s, 2300s, 2500s, 2700s round), not raw tallies. Consistent with aggregator role.
  NOTE: the CLI --ascher flag couldn't match UR269 to KH0506 automatically (minor bug in _okr_to_kh_id); the direct AscherGraph.from_kfg call works.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE
  9-cord EMPTY opening (p1-p9) -- unique in the analyzed Incahuasi set.
  Heavy L1 cords at p14/p18/p19/p23/p24 (opening compound-records with 2 subsidiaries each).
  Paired W/NB body (p45-p250): ~200 cord-pairs of (W=gross, NB=net).
  Paired-W/NB deduction pattern (tax formula):
    p70 W=2700 / p71 NB=2600 (diff 100)
    p73 W=2810 / p74 NB=2700 (diff 110)
    p83 W=2460 / p84 NB=2418 (diff 42)
    p89 W=2633 / p90 NB=2500 (diff 133)
    p112 W=2000 / p113 NB=2000 (diff 0)
    ~200 more pairs -- the longest direct demonstration of Urton-Chu's formula in the corpus.
  Two long closing empty blocks: p251-264 (14 empties), p277-289 (13 empties).

VOCABULARY (9 words, v1-syllabic on KFG-merged knots)
  piqa x4 (summit / who-TOP -- provenance query)
  taqa x4 (boundary / frontier)
  chaqa x3 (bridge / cut)
  maqa x2 (strike)
  chiqa x2 (verification / boundary marker)
  qaqa x2 (rock / landmark)
  waqa x2 (huaca)
  paqa x1 (east orientation)
  chaqaqa x1 (bridge-TOP -- the 'main' bridge?)
  SIGNATURE: 7/8 cadastral markers. Missing kaqa (wall), siqa (ramp -- not expected at Incahuasi).
  NOTABLE ABSENCES vs UR270: no chama (food), no pama (formula), no taki (chant), no paki (break), no pima (who-DIR). The RITUAL/COMMODITY layer of UR270 Zone 3 is entirely absent. Consistent with aggregator role: the individual ceremonies are already bundled into sums.

VOCAB POSITIONS (chapter markers)
  p80, p102, p187, p235 = piqa (who) -- four anchors
  p106, p213, p215, p234 = taqa (boundary) -- four sub-regions
  p134, p153, p242 = chaqa (bridges/cuts)
  p140, p224 = qaqa (rocks)
  p180, p200 = waqa (huacas)
  p190 = chaqaqa (main bridge, topic-marked)
  p135, p232 = chiqa (verification points)
  p103, p240 = maqa (closing strikes)
  p141 = paqa (east orientation)
  Vocabulary threads through the body every 10-20 cords, indexing the provenance of each deposit cluster.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  W (81) = TOTAL / GROSS DEPOSIT + provenance labels (43% of vocab occurrences)
  NB (106) = NET AFTER TAX (paired partner of W; 33% of vocab -- event-verbs chaqa, maqa on NB)
  AB (64) = opening compound-records + secondary numerical
  MB (52) = medium-tier totals + chiqa verification marker

HYPOTHESIS
  UR269 is the intermediate tier of the three-tier peanut tribute pipeline at Incahuasi. It aggregates ~100 regional peanut deposits in paired W(gross)/NB(net) format implementing Urton-Chu's tax formula at the cord-pair level. The vocabulary indexes the PROVENANCE of each deposit (which locality, which landmark, which huaca, which bridge) without the ritual framing of UR270. The 4-level Merkle cascade and the 10 inter-group sums reported by KFG confirm a HIERARCHICAL AUDIT TRAIL structure, narrow-and-tall, consistent with a middle administrator who needs to verify tier-1 warehouse counts and carry them forward to tier-3 district totals.

THREE-TIER SYSTEM
  Tier 1: UR270 (UE 13) -- warehouse day-book with liturgical opening (pama/chama/taki), real counts, Benford 0.83
  Tier 2: UR269 (UE 11) -- THIS KHIPU -- regional aggregator, paired W/NB tax formula, quota values, Benford 0.57, deepest Merkle cascade
  Tier 3: UR268 (UE 10 or similar) -- district summary (grand total ~556,242 per 2026-03 contribution, not re-read with v3 yet)

DOCUMENT TYPE: commodity_accounting (OVERRIDE of v3 classifier's cadastral_survey)
  The v3 classifier caught the 7/8 cadastral markers but missed the Urton-Chu tax-formula signature at cord-pair level and the nickname 'Quipu del Mani'. Keep commodity_accounting to preserve the three-tier framing and the nickname.

CROSS-CORPUS
  UR270 (UE 13) -- Tier 1 companion, same nickname, same complex, same formula implementation, complementary (has liturgy, UR269 doesn't).
  UR268 -- presumed Tier 3, needs v3 re-read.
  UR277 (UE 17), UR278 (UE 16) -- same complex, different functions (map, calendar).
  UR144 -- parallel fixed-value formula (kama x15 with S=7 constant).

CONFIDENCE
  Word reading: HIGH (20/21 dict-confirmed, 95%).
  Document type: HIGH (commodity_accounting with cadastral provenance layer -- overriding classifier).
  Narrative: MEDIUM-HIGH (three-tier hypothesis internally coherent and grounded in Urton-Chu 2015; UR268 v3 re-read needed for full confirmation).
  Data integrity: LOW (0/17 Ascher verified, covering 0.16 LOW, Benford 0.57 LOW) -- but each of these is a diagnostic fingerprint of the aggregator role, not a failure.
